title: FFI: Calling C from Rust
content:
Foreign Function Interface (FFI) lets Rust call C libraries and lets C call Rust functions. This is one of the most common uses of unsafe: the compiler cannot verify the safety of foreign code.

Use extern "C" to declare C functions, #[repr(C)] for C-compatible struct layouts, and handle C strings with CStr/CString. The bindgen tool can automatically generate Rust bindings from C headers.
keyPoints: extern "C" declares or defines functions using the C calling convention
#[repr(C)] ensures struct layout matches C's layout rules
CString owns a C-compatible string; CStr borrows one
#[no_mangle] prevents name mangling so C can find the function
Always check for null pointers when receiving from C
Use bindgen to auto-generate bindings from C header files
